name: lru_beats_fifo
description: Pages 0 and 1 are reused after loading; FIFO evicts them (oldest arrivals) while LRU keeps them (recently used).

name: lru_advantage_hot_cold
description: Pages 0-2 are a hot working set; pages 3 and 4 are one-time cold intruders. FIFO evicts hot pages (oldest arrivals); LRU and TwoList protect them.

name: twolist_approximation_cost
description: TwoList promotes pages 0 and 1 to active after two accesses, then protects them past their useful lifetime; it evicts the wrong page when space is needed, costing one extra fault vs LRU.

name: frequency_locality
description: Realistic Zipf-like workload: page 0 is very hot, pages 1-2 are warm, pages 3-5 are cold. LRU's recency tracking nearly halves the fault rate versus FIFO.

name: working_set_shift
description: A program's working set gradually shifts across three phases (pages 0-4, then 2-6, then 4-8), each phase accessed 25 times. With enough capacity all algorithms adapt equally well.

name: beladys_anomaly_3frames
description: Classic Belady sequence with 3 frames: compare with beladys_anomaly_4frames to see FIFO produce MORE faults with MORE memory.

name: beladys_anomaly_4frames
description: Same sequence as beladys_anomaly_3frames with 4 frames: FIFO gets 10 faults (worse than 3 frames!); LRU is immune and improves to 8.

name: thrashing
description: Working set is 5 pages but only 4 frames are available; every reference faults for all algorithms.

name: repeated_scan_resistance
description: Hot working set (pages 0-3) is re-accessed between three successive 8-page one-time scans. FIFO and LRU lose the hot pages to each scan; TwoList keeps them in the active list across all three scans.

name: all_hits_after_cold_start
description: Working set of 3 pages fits within capacity of 4; after the initial cold faults every subsequent reference is a hit for all algorithms.

name: single_frame
description: Only one frame available; every reference to a new page evicts the single page. All algorithms should behave identically and fault on every unique page transition.
